key_points: The story is narrated by a young thief named Hari Singh
Hari Singh earns his living by stealing and changing his name frequently
Anil is a kind, simple, and trusting writer
Hari Singh steals money from Anil but feels guilty
Education and trust play an important role in Hari Singh’s transformation
Anil forgives Hari Singh silently and continues to teach him
characters: Hari Singh – a young thief who reforms
Anil – a kind and trusting writer
symbols: Wet fifty-rupee note – symbol of forgiveness and awareness
Rain – symbol of guilt and inner conflict
exam_tips: Remember that the story uses first-person narration
Focus on Anil’s silent forgiveness in answers
Highlight the theme of reform rather than punishment
Use examples like the wet note to explain symbolism
